skill.name: testing-patterns
skill.description: Jest testing patterns, factory functions, mocking strategies, and TDD workflow. Use when writing unit tests, creating test factories, or following TDD red-green-refactor cycle.
skill.summary: Comprehensive Jest testing patterns including factory functions, mocking strategies, custom render utilities, and TDD workflow for writing maintainable tests.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Security review completed. All 42 static findings are false positives triggered by the static analyzer misinterpreting: (1) markdown code formatting backticks as shell commands, (2) TypeScript generics like Partial<X> as cryptographic patterns, and (3) the word 'APIs' as network reconnaissance. The skill is legitimate Jest testing documentation with no security concerns.

content.value_statement: This skill provides ready-to-use Jest testing patterns including factory functions, custom render utilities, and mocking strategies that help developers write maintainable, DRY tests following TDD principles.
actual_capabilities: Create reusable factory functions for test data and component props
Set up custom render functions with providers for React components
Implement proper mocking strategies for modules and GraphQL hooks
Follow TDD red-green-refactor workflow with failing tests first
Structure tests with describe blocks for organized test suites
Avoid common anti-patterns like testing mocks instead of real behavior
limitations: Skill focuses on Jest and React Testing Library patterns only
Does not cover end-to-end testing frameworks like Cypress or Playwright
Assumes basic familiarity with JavaScript/TypeScript and testing concepts
Does not include specific backend testing patterns

use_cases.title: Setting up test infrastructure
use_cases.description: Create custom render functions and factory utilities that wrap components with required providers like ThemeProvider, reducing boilerplate in every test.

use_cases.title: Writing maintainable unit tests
use_cases.description: Use factory functions to generate consistent test data, making tests easier to read, maintain, and update when data structures change.
use_cases.target_user: QA engineers and developers writing test suites

use_cases.title: Following TDD methodology
use_cases.description: Apply the red-green-refactor cycle by writing failing tests first, then implementing minimal code to pass, ensuring tests drive development.

prompt_templates.title: Basic test setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a basic test file for a React component. Show me how to create a custom render function that wraps the component with a ThemeProvider.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with testing a new component

prompt_templates.title: Factory function for data
prompt_templates.description: Create a factory function for generating mock user data with sensible defaults
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a factory function called getMockUser that returns a user object with default values for id, name, email, and role. Show how to override specific properties using Partial type.

prompt_templates.title: Mocking a module
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to mock a module like 'utils/analytics' in Jest. Include how to access the mock in the test and how to mock return values.

prompt_templates.title: TDD workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk me through the TDD workflow for adding a new feature. Start with writing a failing test, then show the minimal implementation to pass it.
prompt_templates.scenario: Applying test-driven development to new features

output_examples.input: Create a factory function for user data
Show me a custom render function
How should I structure my tests?
output_examples.output: Use describe blocks to group related tests: describe('ComponentName', () => { describe('Rendering', () => {...}); describe('User interactions', () => {...}); describe('Edge cases', () => {...}); });

best_practices: Always use factory functions for props and data to keep tests DRY and consistent
Test behavior through the public API rather than implementation details
Use descriptive test names that explain what behavior is being tested
anti_patterns: Avoid testing mock behavior instead of actual component behavior
Do not duplicate test data across tests - use factory functions instead
Avoid testing implementation details that may change refactoring

faq.question: What is the factory pattern in testing?
faq.answer: The factory pattern uses functions like getMockUser() that return test data with sensible defaults. You can override specific properties as needed, keeping tests DRY and maintainable.

faq.question: How do I create a custom render function?
faq.answer: Create a function that wraps the standard render with required providers. For example, renderWithTheme wraps your component with ThemeProvider so you do not repeat this in every test.

faq.question: What is the TDD workflow?
faq.answer: TDD stands for Test-Driven Development. Follow three steps: Red (write a failing test), Green (write minimal code to pass), and Refactor (improve code while keeping tests passing).

faq.question: How do I mock a module in Jest?
faq.answer: Use jest.mock('module-path') before your tests. You can provide a factory function to specify what the module should return. Access mocks using jest.requireMock().

faq.question: What should I test: implementation or behavior?
faq.answer: Test behavior through the public API. Avoid testing internal state or implementation details because these may change during refactoring while the external behavior stays the same.

faq.question: How do I organize large test suites?
faq.answer: Use describe blocks to group tests by category: Rendering, User interactions, and Edge cases. This makes tests easier to read and maintain.
